books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: A psychological thriller that follows Raskolnikov, a poor student who murders a pawnbroker, exploring themes of morality and redemption in 19th century Russia.
books.historical_context: Written during a time of great social and political upheaval in Russia, reflecting the period's philosophical debates about morality and individual will.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.summary: A witty examination of the manners, marriage, and social class in Georgian England through the story of Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy.
books.historical_context: Set during the Regency era in England, depicting the social conventions and expectations of the landed gentry.

books.title: Don Quixote
books.author: Miguel de Cervantes
books.summary: A parody of chivalric romances, following the adventures of an elderly nobleman who loses his sanity and becomes a knight-errant.
books.historical_context: Written during Spain's Golden Age, satirizing the medieval chivalric tradition and reflecting the transition to the modern novel.

books.title: The Canterbury Tales
books.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
books.summary: A collection of stories told by pilgrims traveling to Canterbury Cathedral, providing a rich tapestry of medieval English life.
books.historical_context: Written during the Middle Ages, offering insights into the social structure and daily life of medieval England.

books.author: George Orwell
books.summary: A dystopian novel about a totalitarian regime that maintains power through surveillance, manipulation of language, and control of information.
books.historical_context: Written in the aftermath of World War II, reflecting fears about totalitarianism and the rise of surveillance states.

books.title: Romeo and Juliet
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: A tragedy about two young star-crossed lovers whose deaths ultimately reconcile their feuding families.
books.historical_context: Written during the Elizabethan era, reflecting Renaissance themes and the political tensions of the time.

books.title: Gulliver's Travels
books.author: Jonathan Swift
books.summary: A satire on human nature and a parody of the 'travelers' tales' literary genre, following the misadventures of Lemuel Gulliver.
books.historical_context: Published during the Enlightenment era, satirizing European governments, politics, religion, and human nature.

books.title: The Prince
books.author: Niccolò Machiavelli
books.summary: A political treatise on political power, the acquisition, and the retention of it, portraying that the end justifies the means.
books.historical_context: Written during the Italian Renaissance, reflecting the political turmoil and the city-state politics of Italy at the time.

books.title: Robinson Crusoe
books.author: Daniel Defoe
books.summary: The story of a man shipwrecked on a deserted island and his struggle for survival and eventual redemption.
books.historical_context: Published during the Age of Exploration, reflecting colonial expansion and the individual's relationship with nature and God.

books.title: Divine Comedy
books.author: Dante Alighieri
books.summary: An epic poem that narrates Dante's journey through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise, serving as an allegory of the soul's journey towards God.
books.historical_context: Written during the Late Middle Ages, reflecting the political and religious climate of Florence and Italy during the time.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.summary: A novel about a young scientist who creates a sapient creature in an unorthodox scientific experiment, exploring themes of ambition, responsibility, and the consequences of playing God.
books.historical_context: Written during the Romantic period, influenced by the Industrial Revolution and scientific advancements of the time.

books.title: The Decameron
books.author: Giovanni Boccaccio
books.summary: A collection of novellas containing 100 tales told by a group of young people sheltering in a secluded villa just outside Florence to escape the Black Death.
books.historical_context: Written in the aftermath of the Black Death pandemic that devastated Europe, reflecting the social impact of the plague.

books.title: Utopia
books.author: Thomas More
books.summary: A work of fiction and socio-political satire that pictures an ideal society on an imaginary island, contrasting it with European society of the day.
books.historical_context: Written during the Renaissance and the Age of Discovery, reflecting the social and political issues of Tudor England.

books.title: Candide
books.author: Voltaire
books.summary: A satirical novella that follows the adventures of a young man, Candide, who is living a sheltered life and being indoctrinated with optimism.
books.historical_context: Written during the Age of Enlightenment, criticizing the religious and political establishments of the time.

books.title: Brave New World
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.summary: A dystopian novel that anticipates developments in reproductive technology, sleep-learning, psychological manipulation, and classical conditioning that combine to change society.
books.historical_context: Written between the World Wars, reflecting anxieties about technological advancement and totalitarianism.
